proposed_from.topic: Proximal partial identification of the simple-mediation natural/separable direct effect (and the nested recanting-witness path-specific effect, delegated) when proximal completeness fails: the exact fixed-cardinality factorization-constrained proximal response-function program gives a valid, observed-data-computable negative-control-proxy bound that strictly refines the no-proxy Breum/Sjolander floor; anchor 2605.09462 (proximal point-ID under completeness), refine 2512.11549 no-proxy floor, non-reduction vs Ghassami 2304.04374 single-world ATE
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The focal object is a robust positive-cell strict contraction in a graph-native compatible-SCM fiber; the response-function program is supporting machinery and the recanting target is delegated.","published_axis":"The strict endpoint-exclusion target tightens the no-proxy natural-effect floor of Sjölander2009 and BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 under a failed-completeness proxy factorization. GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 includes hidden-mediator cross-world proxy bounds but not this observed-mediator response-type coupling."}
seed_list: A fixed-\(K\) proxy-factorized response-function program is the sharp natural-direct-effect set and detects strict contraction of the no-proxy interval by endpoint-face infeasibility.
The fixed-\(K\) proxy-factorized natural-direct-effect hierarchy either stabilizes at an explicit observable-alphabet cardinality or admits a strict \(K\)-versus-\(K+1\) witness.
A nested recanting-witness path-specific effect has a sharp proxy-factorized response-function set after one or more bridge completeness conditions fail.
Estimated proxy-factorized mediation-bound endpoints admit uniformly valid confidence sets near rank boundaries and tied optimizers.
Two positive proxy laws with the same bridge quotient certificate can have distinct sharp causal sets because only one null direction is latent-realizable.
A natural direct-effect proxy set reduces to single-world proxy bounds if and only if an explicit cross-world response-type compatibility condition holds.
The joint natural and separable direct-effect proxy set reveals when their coincident no-proxy intervals separate after proxy factorization.
For binary proxies and \(K=2\), signed minors and ranks characterize when a proxy-factorized model excludes a no-proxy direct-effect endpoint.

seed_details.one_line: A fixed-\(K\) proxy-factorized response-function program is the sharp natural-direct-effect set and detects strict contraction of the no-proxy interval by endpoint-face infeasibility.

anchor_paper.author: Bai et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Proximal bridges point-identify path-specific effects under completeness.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M20 is graphical because the feasible class is a latent SCM with cross-world response types, not a generic observed-law LP.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from Duarte et al. because any discrete SCM already has a polynomial program.
seed_details.rebuttal: Duarte et al. do not encode this negative-control factorization or the natural-direct-effect cross-world target, and their generic result does not establish the no-proxy frontier.

adversarial_referee.instance: The factorization assumes sharpness.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It only specifies the latent SCM; endpoint attainment is a theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: Breum already uses latent response types.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The claim is proxy-factorized refinement, not a replacement for its floor.

adversarial_referee.instance: This is merely generic polynomial optimization.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The new object is the graph-constrained cross-world proxy set and its endpoint-lift frontier.

seed_details.one_line: The fixed-\(K\) proxy-factorized natural-direct-effect hierarchy either stabilizes at an explicit observable-alphabet cardinality or admits a strict \(K\)-versus-\(K+1\) witness.

anchor_paper.author: Duarte et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Finite response functions can encode a discrete SCM without loss at sufficient cardinality.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M6 needs a genuine support-reduction theorem or a nondegenerate separation, not a routine support increase.
seed_details.referee_objection: A Caratheodory bound makes this automatic.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proxy kernels impose nonconvex factorization ranks, so ordinary convex support reduction does not directly preserve the model.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only a sufficient cardinality cap is likely.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Keep only if necessity or a strict witness is produced.

adversarial_referee.instance: A single rank-zero example would be uninformative.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Require strictly positive observed cells and a relative-open proxy-rank regime.

adversarial_referee.instance: Duarte studies unrestricted cardinality.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The exact gap is stabilization under additional factorization constraints.

seed_details.one_line: A nested recanting-witness path-specific effect has a sharp proxy-factorized response-function set after one or more bridge completeness conditions fail.

anchor_paper.author: Bai et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Proximal bridges identify a nested path-specific effect under completeness.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M20 retains the recanting-witness SCM rather than importing a single-world proxy bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: It is only a larger version of the simple mediation program.
seed_details.rebuttal: The recanting witness creates conflicting nested assignments and district-level cross-world compatibility.

adversarial_referee.instance: The nested counterfactual family is not specified.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Must enumerate response types before advancing.

adversarial_referee.instance: The program may be computationally intractable.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Delegate until a small finite graph is fixed.

adversarial_referee.instance: Completeness failure is not an authored open question.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The published paper supplies point identification but not a residual set.

seed_details.one_line: Estimated proxy-factorized mediation-bound endpoints admit uniformly valid confidence sets near rank boundaries and tied optimizers.

anchor_paper.author: Breum et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp mediation bounds are derived without an inference procedure.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M20+M8 requires a completed identified-set object before endpoint inference is meaningful.
seed_details.referee_objection: General intersection-bound tools already cover nonsmooth endpoints.
seed_details.rebuttal: Estimated factorization rank changes the feasible set under local proxy weakness.

adversarial_referee.instance: No endpoint estimator is defined.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Requires the chosen program first.

adversarial_referee.instance: Uniformity could be assumed through a rank gap.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Do not retain a theorem that assumes away weak proxies.

adversarial_referee.instance: Breum does not use proxies.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Its stated inferential gap motivates the mediated endpoint layer.

seed_details.one_line: Two positive proxy laws with the same bridge quotient certificate can have distinct sharp causal sets because only one null direction is latent-realizable.

anchor_paper.author: Zhang et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Nonunique bridge solutions can still identify a causal functional.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M7 requires a positive relative-open law family, not a hand-tuned table.
seed_details.referee_objection: A failed banked witness suggests the phenomenon may be false.
seed_details.rebuttal: The missing ingredient was a factorization-constrained primal completion and dual obstruction, not a negative theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: A zero cell produces the separation.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Require all observed cells to be positive.

adversarial_referee.instance: Quotient identity is not a causal-set theorem.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposition is explicitly about latent-realizable causal sets.

adversarial_referee.instance: No rational witness is known.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Downgrade unless a solved \(K=2\) table is obtained.

seed_details.one_line: A natural direct-effect proxy set reduces to single-world proxy bounds if and only if an explicit cross-world response-type compatibility condition holds.

anchor_paper.author: Ghassami et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Proxy variables bound causal effects without completeness for single-world targets.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M6 must establish both a reduction construction and an incompatible cross-world countermodel.
seed_details.referee_objection: Natural effects are visibly cross-world, so nonreduction is tautological.
seed_details.rebuttal: The novelty is an exact necessary-and-sufficient condition for when the coupling becomes redundant.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only sufficient separability conditions may be found.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Drop the iff claim without a necessity construction.

adversarial_referee.instance: Ghassami also treats hidden mediators.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It still does not provide a natural-effect cross-world reduction.

adversarial_referee.instance: Compatibility is undefined.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Define it by response-type couplings, not bridge nullspaces.

seed_details.one_line: The joint natural and separable direct-effect proxy set reveals when their coincident no-proxy intervals separate after proxy factorization.

anchor_paper.author: Breum et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Analogous natural and separable bounds coincide without proxies.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The comparison must use the published separable intervention, not a relabelled natural response type.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a notational comparison of two effects.
seed_details.rebuttal: Proxy factorization can constrain their distinct cross-world completions differently.

adversarial_referee.instance: The separable model has extra intervention structure.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Encode its treatment components explicitly before retaining.

adversarial_referee.instance: The two response families are not aligned.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use a common SCM only when scientifically justified.

adversarial_referee.instance: The joint program is much larger.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use it as a follow-on to the simple NDE core.

seed_details.one_line: For binary proxies and \(K=2\), signed minors and ranks characterize when a proxy-factorized model excludes a no-proxy direct-effect endpoint.

anchor_paper.author: Bai et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Categorical completeness depends on proxy richness relative to the latent factor.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M6 is viable only if signed-minor conditions are necessary as well as sufficient.
seed_details.referee_objection: Quantifier elimination makes any finite characterization formal but unusable.
seed_details.rebuttal: The target is a low-degree rank-and-minor diagnostic derived from the endpoint faces, not generic elimination output.

adversarial_referee.instance: Minors might merely certify one subset.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State necessity as the open content.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only a rank-deficient boundary is covered.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Require strictly positive cells and nontrivial proxy rank.

adversarial_referee.instance: This is algebra rather than a causal result.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It characterizes a causal endpoint-lift event.

proposed_from.literature_map:
BaiWuSunCui2026 develops four bridge strategies for a path-specific effect and root-\(n\) inference, but completeness is load-bearing.
BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 gives sharp no-proxy natural and separable mediation bounds and identifies no inference result.
GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 gives proxy bounds without completeness for ATE and ETT, not cross-world natural direct effects.
DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 represents discrete SCMs by response functions and certifies global polynomial-program extrema.
ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 distinguishes nonunique bridges from nonidentified functionals.
MilesKankiMeloniTchetgen2017 identifies the cross-world restrictions specific to pure direct effects.
Sjolander2009 provides the randomized-treatment no-proxy direct-effect floor.
The surviving gap is the joint latent-realizable proxy-factorized response set for a cross-world direct effect when completeness fails.
The chosen technique adapts the response-function construction of DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 and the mediation motivation arc of BaiWuSunCui2026.
No located paper combines fixed latent cardinality, proxy factorization, and the natural-direct-effect response type into a sharp observed-data program.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:robust-strict-contraction
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The result is conditional on the fixed three-state positive-mass NPSEM, factorized two-proxy measurement model, shared coarsening, and quantitative separation; it makes no unrestricted-cardinality or point-identification claim.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any certified strict contraction is only over \(\mathfrak M_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}\) after the SCM/program correspondence is explicitly defined.

banked_reason: Stopped by user after the D-1 proposal gate failed to converge: angle 2 received a hard not-publishable repo-subsumption reject, and the angle-3 gluing-frontier reframe remained unaccepted.
banked_proposal_promise_gap: The requested field-tier kernel never cleared D-1 after 16 review attempts; the final gluing-frontier reframe remained at REVISE when stopped.
